paper_title: Methods used at Gomel Institute of Pathology

methods.description: Process for measuring the specific activity of caesium (Cs-137) in humans using gamma rays, which are less radiotoxic than beta rays. This method is used to assess the level of Cs-137 accumulated in various organs.

steps.description: Use different equipment to measure the level of Cs-137 accumulated in various organs during whole body measurement or autopsies.
steps.searchable_summary: measure Cs-137 levels in human organs using gamma ray equipment

steps.description: Ensure accuracy of measurements by annual state inspection of the equipment used by the Belrad Institute.
steps.searchable_summary: perform annual state inspection for equipment accuracy

steps.description: Verify equipment accuracy through intercalibrations as part of a joint German-Belarussian project.

methods.method_name: Laboratory Measurement of Specific Activity in Samples
methods.description: Laboratory procedure for measuring the specific activity of Cs-137 in samples, such as organs examined during autopsy, using automated gamma radiometers.

steps.description: Use automated Rug-92M gamma radiometers to measure specific activity in samples.
steps.searchable_summary: measure Cs-137 activity in samples using Rug-92M gamma radiometers

steps.description: Set measurement duration to one minute for samples >100 Bq/kg and 10 minutes for samples of 50-100 Bq/kg.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust measurement duration based on sample activity level

steps.description: Double-check samples in France to validate the findings.
steps.searchable_summary: validate sample measurements by double-checking in France
